{{Quote|Suddenly, the ''Tantive IV'' was the most wanted ship in the galaxy.|Excerpt from "'''Raymus'''"|<ref name="FACPOV">{{StoryCite|story=Raymus|book=From a Certain Point of View}}</ref>}}
"'''Raymus'''" is the first short story in the anthology ''[[From a Certain Point of View]]''. The story, written by [[Gary Whitta]], focuses on the point of view of [[Captain]] [[Raymus Antilles]] and bridges the brief gap between the final scene of ''[[Rogue One: A Star Wars Story]]'' and the opening scenes of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. Whitta had previously also written the initial first draft for ''Rogue One''.

==Plot summary==
After fleeing the [[Battle of Scarif]], the ''[[Tantive IV]]'' is barely maintaining [[lightspeed]] due to a malfunctioning [[hyperdrive]]. Aboard the ship as it is being furiously hunted by the [[Galactic Empire|Empire]], Captain Raymus Antilles gives the [[datacard]] containing [[Death Star plans|the plans]] for the first [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Star]] to [[Prince]]ss [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]] as they travel to [[Tatooine]]. Moments after Raymus prepares the [[escape pod]]s, the hyperdrive fails and the ship exits [[hyperspace]], only about a quarter [[parsec]] away from Tatooine. The ''[[Devastator]]'', a [[Star Destroyer]] carrying [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]], tracks the ship's abnormal hyperspace wake and locks on to the ''Tantive IV'' with its tractor beam. As [[Imperial Military|Imperial]] troops board the ship, Raymus attempts to convince Princess Leia to escape after she gives the datacard to [[R2-D2]]. Leia refuses, and she and R2 go to help others escape. Raymus is then captured by [[stormtrooper]]s and brought to Darth Vader. After Raymus refuses to disclose any information about the Death Star plans, Darth Vader murders him by lifting him off the ground by his throat, crushing his windpipe.
